typecheck.command: xcodebuild build-for-testing -project /Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar/OpenBurnBar.xcodeproj -scheme OpenBurnBar -destination platform=macOS,arch=arm64 -clonedSourcePackagesDirPath /Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar/.spm-cache -derivedDataPath /Users/albertonunez/Documents/Windsurf/BurnBar/.derived-data/ci-typecheck CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO CODE_SIGNING_REQUIRED=NO

blockingIssues.description: Reconciliation candidates currently include all completed/failed nodes (including upstream prerequisites) instead of limiting winner selection to conflicting terminal outcomes, so a non-terminal prerequisite can be incorrectly chosen as the winner.

Earliest terminal sequence tie-break uses static DAG declaration order (`terminalNodes.firstIndex`) instead of actual terminal completion sequence, violating replay-stable precedence semantics and allowing incorrect winner/reason selection.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add an explicit scrutiny-facing checklist item that handoff claims `followedProcedure: true` only when all required validator surfaces listed by the skill have been executed and recorded in handoff evidence.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m12-reconciler-winner-selection handoff marked `followedProcedure: true` while omitting required BurnBarRunServiceTests and BurnBarProviderRouterTests from recorded commands.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that when a feature fulfills a cross-surface assertion (e.g., VAL-CROSS-*), handoff evidence must include every required surface command from verificationSteps (daemon + app + extension where listed) before the feature can be marked complete.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature m12-reconciler-winner-selection fulfills VAL-CROSS-006 but recorded daemon/core-only evidence and omitted app/extension validation commands despite mission guidance requiring cross-surface artifacts.
